data.title: Hyundai, Toyota reshape hydrogen vehicle market with new models
data.description: Hyundai Motor Co. is set to launch a second-generation hydrogen model, while Toyota Motor Corp. expands its hydrogen-powered lineup to reshape the fuel cell electric vehicle (FCEV) market. According to industry sources on Thursday, the FCEV sector is entering a new phase with the introduction of new models and advancements in third-generation hydrogen fuel cell systems. It has been more than a decade since Hyundai introduced the world’s first mass-produced hydrogen fuel cell vehicle, the Tucson ix FCEV, in 2013, followed by Toyota’s Mirai in 2014. Toyota has been particularly active in recent years. In 2020, it launched the second-generation Mirai with a larger body and extended driving range. The company introduced a hydrogen-powered Crown model in early 2024, marking a shift from dedicated FCEVs to integrating fuel cell technology into a long-established mainstream lineup that also includes internal combustion and hybrid powertrains. Hyundai, which has not released a new hydrogen model or generational update since the first Nexo’s debut in 2018, is set to counter with the second-generation Nexo in 2025. According to energy market research firm SNE Research, Hyundai remained the global FCEV sales leader in 2024, selling 3,836 units of the Nexo and its hydrogen bus, Elec City. However, sales declined 23.5 percent year-on-year, largely due to weak domestic demand amid the absence of a new Nexo model. In response, Hyundai unveiled the second-generation Nexo concept car, Initium, in October 2024. While the FCEV market has stagnated, hydrogen vehicle sales have been growing in China, particularly in the commercial segment, accounting for 55.3 percent of global FCEV sales in 2024 with 7,113 units sold. Hyundai and Toyota have been competing in the hydrogen vehicle market for a decade, but as overall market growth remains a challenge, they are also strengthening cooperation. Hyundai Motor Executive Chair Euisun Chung and Toyota Chair Akio Toyoda met in November at the 2024 World Rally Championship in Japan. Hyundai Motor Group also launched its hydrogen value chain brand, HTWO, in 2024.

data.title: Kakao founder Kim Beom-su to resign as CA Council co-chair
data.description: Kakao Corp. founder Kim Beom-su will step down as co-chair of the Corporate Alignment (CA) Council, its top decision-making body, due to health reasons. The CA Council will now be led solely by Kakao Chief Executive Officer Chung Shin-a. Kakao announced on Thursday that Kim would be resigning from his positions as co-chair of the CA Council and chairman of the Council’s Management Reform Committee. This decision comes after he was recently diagnosed with early-stage bladder cancer and needs to focus on treatment for the time being. Kim returned to the forefront of management in November 2023, when he took on the role of Management Reform Committee chairman. The appointment came amid internal and external challenges including regulatory investigations into allegations of stock price manipulation related to Kakao’s acquisition of SM Entertainment Co. and internal conflicts among executives. In January 2024, he was appointed co-chair of the CA Council alongside then-CEO designate Chung, leading a major structural overhaul of the group by shifting away from its previous decentralized management system. However, his reform efforts came to a halt in July 2024 when he was arrested in connection with the SM acquisition case. Although he was released on bail in October after 101 days in detention, ongoing legal proceedings prevented him from actively engaging in management. With his health concerns nowadding to the situation, he decided to step down from his role as co-chair of the CA Council. With Kim also resigning as chairman of the Management Reform Committee, the committee will be dissolved. Its responsibilities will now be carried forward by various subcommittees, including the Strategy Committee, Responsible Management Committee, ESG Committee, and Brand Committee, which comprise CEOs of key Kakao subsidiaries. However, Kim will continue to serve as the head of Kakao’s Future Initiative Center, which responsible for shaping the group’s vision and long-term strategy. In the meantime, Kakao also announced on Thursday that it will spin off its portal service, Daum, which currently operates as an in-house independent company. The specific timeline and details of the spinoff have not been disclosed yet.

data.title: Hyundai Mobis aims to expand global customers
data.description: South Korea’s Hyundai Mobis Co. has expressed its commitment to developing top-tier technology to become the world’s leading auto parts company. The company announced Thursday that it held a vision declaration event on Wednesday at its research center in Yongin, Gyeonggi Province, attended by around 300 executives and employees, including Chief Executive Officer Lee Gyu-suk. The new vision is to “lead the transformation of the future automotive industry and push the boundaries of innovation to expand in the global market.” Under this vision, Hyundai Mobis has set three key themes: pioneering mobility innovation, expanding global customers, and limitless growth. Pioneering mobility innovation focuses on securing breakthrough technologies. The company aims to strengthen its position in software-defined vehicles (SDVs), human-machine interfaces (HMIs), electric vehicles (EVs), and chassis safety components, delivering differentiated solutions. Expanding global customers targets increasing the share of overseas revenue from the current 10 percent to 40 percent by 2033. To achieve this, Hyundai Mobis will continue refining its business structure with a focus on profitability. Lee emphasized that establishing a vision is only the first step toward meaningful change, calling for a company-wide effort to bring it to fruition.

data.title: Kakao faces restrictions on data use in AI agent project
data.description: Kakao Corp.faced data-use challenges with its artificial intelligence (AI) agent, Kanana, which is being developed in partnership with OpenAI. The challenges come after the Personal Information Protection Commission (PIPC) ruled that OpenAI cannot use personal data from chat conversations. The PIPC announced the results of a Kanana preliminary review on Thursday. Kakao had requested the review due to concerns about the potential inclusion of personal information, such as account and credit card details in chat conversations, as Kanana is designed to leverage both Kakao’s large language model (LLM) and OpenAI’s ChatGPT for enhanced functionality. Kakao announced its strategic partnership with OpenAI to co-develop an AI agent capable of understanding conversation context and providing relevant responses in February 2025. However, the PIPC’s ruling limits how Kanana can use personal data, ensuring that any data handling remains within strict boundaries. Under Korea’s privacy law, businesses may only collect and use personal information when deemed “necessary” for “performing a contract concluded with the data subject or concluding a contract.” If personal data is not required for such purposes, explicit consent from the user is mandatory. The rationale is that since Kakao is responsible for managing user data and OpenAI can only process encrypted data within Kakao’s defined scope, OpenAI would not be permitted to independently store or use the data even if ChatGPT were to process it. A PIPC official confirmed that the commission has required explicit language to ensure that OpenAI can only use the data for tasks directly commissioned by Kakao, not for its own business objectives. This restriction is expected to limit OpenAI’s ability to collect valuable data from Kanana, prompting many industry insiders to speculate that the AI service may eventually be monetized.

data.title: Samsung SDS invests $5.36 mn in Mistral AI
data.description: Samsung SDS made an equity investment in Mistral AI, Europe’s leading independent AI lab,in February 2024 according to reports on Thursday. According to Samsung SDS’s 2024 business report, the company acquired a 0.12 percent stake in the world’s fifth-largest AI company, with a book value of approximately 7.8 billion won ($5.36 million). Samsung SDS is currently testing Mistral AI within its proprietary generative AI platform, FabriX, and will determine its future application after internal review. Founded in 2023, Mistral AI is a French AI startup led by Arthur Mensch, a former Google DeepMind researcher. Samsung SDS said it had invested in Mistral AI as part of a broader technology collaboration effort.

data.title: SK hynix components to be tested in space
data.description: SK hynix Inc.’s DRAM and other domestically-produced components and devices will be launched into space aboard South Korea’s Nuri rocket to undergo performance verification. On Thursday, the Korea Aerospace Administration and the Korea Aerospace Research Institute announced the results of the payload selection for the Domestic Components Space Verification Support Program for the second space verification satellite. The space verification program, which runs for four years from 2024 with a budget of 12 billion won, aims to support the verification of domestically developed electrical and electronic components and semiconductors in space. The program will test whether these components function properly in space and assess their resistance to exposure to cosmic radiation. According to the selection results, SK hynix’s DRAM and flash-memory products, including UFS, will be onboard the Nuri, a three-stage launch vehicle developed by the KARI. Other components manufactured by startups were also selected for assessment, including InceraSolutions’ high-speed precision mirrors, Cosmo Bee’s 1A-class hollow cathode for electric thrusters, and InterGravity Technologies‘ orbital transport ship anti-jamming equipment.

data.title: Cuckoo targets $1.38 bn in sales with business diversification
data.description: Cuckoo, which began as a rice cooker specialist in South Korea is seeing strong results from its business diversification strategy. After strengthening its home appliance lineup with robot vacuum cleaners and air purifiers, the company has recently expanded into new product categories such as kimchi refrigerators. Cuckoo Holdings, which includes rental service provider Cuckoo Homesys and manufacturer Cuckoo Electronics, aims to hit 2 trillion won ($1.38 billion) in total sales in 2025. Cuckoo said on Thursday that Cuckoo Homesys recorded 1.05 trillion won in revenue in 2024, a 10.8 percent increase year-over-year, marking the first time it surpassed 1 trillion won in annual sales. With Cuckoo Holdings generating 594.5 billion won in revenue through the third quarter of 2024, total group sales are projected to reach 1.9 trillion won for the year. Cuckoo’s recent product launches underscore its evolution into a comprehensive home appliance brand. In 2025 to date, the company has introduced a two-person electric rice cooker, a microbial food waste processor, the Restino Euro Top mattress and frame, and a cordless hand blender. Cuckoo responded to increasing competition from Chinese brands by launching four consecutive vacuum cleaner models, starting with the Power Clone Slim, in 2024. The company has been diversifying into kitchen appliances for smaller households, rental-based products, and general home electronics. A Cuckoo official said, “We maintain a broad product lineup, ranging from high-end to budget-friendly options across categories such as rice cookers, water purifiers, induction cooktops, and vacuum cleaners. New product launches are carefully evaluated based on requests from retail distribution channels.” A prime example is Cuckoo’s 133L kimchi refrigerator, launched in 2024 in response to retailer demand for a reliable, affordably priced model in the 600,000 won range. Cuckoo’s leadership believes that if low-cost Chinese products dominate the market, consumer choice will shrink significantly, prompting the company to develop products that align with market demand. To reinforce stability in its new businesses, Cuckoo is doubling down on its expertise in “gourmet appliances,” ensuring that its products support every step of the cooking process. The company offers freezers and kimchi refrigerators for food storage, rice cookers, induction cooktops, microwaves, air fryers, and blenders for cooking alongside dishwashers and food waste processors for post-meal cleanup. This strategy has proven successful, with sales of gourmet appliances - including induction cooktops, microwaves, and food waste processors - rising 39 percent year-over-year in 2024, significantly driving overall appliance revenue. Cuckoo is also exploring business-to-business (B2B) expansion. The company recently signed a water treatment facility supply contract with Lotte International, under which its filtration systems will be used in the production of “no-wash rice” - a product that eliminates the need for rinsing before cooking. Cuckoo’s water purification technology was recognized for maintaining rice quality while effectively removing impurities during processing. The B2B rental business is also growing rapidly, with revenue surging 253 percent year-over-year from January to October 2024. Cuckoo has secured major contracts with hotels like Inspire Resort in Incheon and Lotte Hotel La Seine, as well as CJ Logistics O-NE, NH Nonghyup Bank, public institutions, and military bases.

data.title: Samsung SDI to raise $1.38 bn for EV battery supercycle
data.description: Samsung SDI announced a 2 trillion won ($1.38 billion) rights offering to strengthen its long-term competitiveness and accelerate growth, as the electric vehicle (EV) battery market faces a temporary slowdown in demand. The company’s board of directors approved the capital increase on Friday, saying that the funds will be used for expanding production capacity at its joint venture with General Motors (GM) in the United States, scaling up its Hungary plant, and investing in solid-state battery production facilities in South Korea. The rights offering, which will be conducted via a shareholder allocation followed by a public offering of forfeited shares, will issue 11,821,000 new shares, or a 16.8 percent capital increase. Samsung SDI has set several key dates for the rights offering process. The record date for the new share allocation is April 18th, 2025, while the final offer price will be determined on May 22nd. The subscription period will take place between May 27th and June 3rd, during which priority will be given to employee stock ownership associations and existing shareholders, followed by a general subscription for remaining shares. The new shares will then be listed on June 19th. Samsung SDI said that the capital increase aligns with the EV battery industry’s long-term growth prospects, noting that battery production requires a 2-to-3-year lead time from facility investment to mass production. Global automakers remain committed to long-term electrification strategies despite the slowdown in EV market growth. Market research firms project that the EV battery market will sustain an annual growth rate of around 20 percent between 2025 and 2030. To secure its future growth drivers, Samsung SDI is advancing joint ventures with Stellantis and GM in the United States, expanding Hungary’s production facilities, and investing in next-generation battery technologies, including solid-state batteries and lithium iron phosphate (LFP) batteries. The company’s capital expenditures have surged almost fourfold from 1.7 trillion won in 2019 to 6.6 trillion won in 2024. While investment is expected to decline slightly in 2025 due to temporary market conditions, Samsung SDI reaffirmed its commitment to securing future technologies and production capacity. Samsung SDI emphasized that the rights offering is a proactive measure to secure funding for long-term investments and maintain financial stability, ensuring market leadership when demand rebounds. The company is also exploring other funding options, including asset utilization, to diversify its capital-raising strategies.

data.title: Hyundai Steel labor, management wage talks collapse again
data.description: The wage negotiations between Hyundai Steel Co.’s labor and management have broken down once again, showing no signs of resolution. While the steel company under South Korea’s auto giant Hyundai Motor Group offered performance-based bonuses at the cost of incurring losses, its labor union continues to demand wage increases that are on par with Hyundai Motor Co., making it difficult for the two sides to reach an agreement. According to industry sources on Thursday, Hyundai Steel’s labor and management resumed wage negotiations later in the day but failed to reach a tentative agreement. The union immediately declared that it would resume partial strikes, citing the company’s lack of additional proposals, and plans to go on strike until next Thursday. Hyundai Steel’s labor and management have been holding collective bargaining negotiations since September 2024 but have been unable to reach an agreement. The union has continued acting, with moves including full strikes and partial or temporary walkouts. In response, the company took a hardline stance by partially locking out certain production lines at its cold-rolling plant in the Dangjin Steelworks. Although the company lifted the lockout on Tuesday and both sides resumed negotiations, the talks fell apart once again. Hyundai Steel’s Suncheon plant also decided to stage a partial strike, with workers stopping work for four hours each day on Friday and Saturday.

data.title: Celltrion to retire $140 mn treasury shares
data.description: South Korea’s Celltrion Inc. announced on Friday that it has decided to retire its entire treasury stock acquired this year worth 203.3 billion won ($140 million). The company will cancel 1,101,379 shares, which is equivalent to about 11 percent of its outstanding shares. They are worth 203.3 billion won based on Celltrion’s closing price on Thursday. Once the shares are canceled, the total number of shares issued by Celltrion will decrease to 213,006,740 shares from 214,108,119 shares. Celltrion also plans to retire additional treasury shares it purchases this year. In 2024, the company acquired treasury shares worth 436 billion won. In February 2025, it decided to purchase an additional 100 billion won worth of treasury shares. Celltrion expects that reducing the total number of issued shares through these retirements will naturally increase the value of shareholders’ holdings. “Following our record-breaking revenue of about 3.5 trillion won last year, the highest since the company’s inception, we are accelerating growth with the goal of reaching 5 trillion won in revenue this year,” said an official from Celltrion. “In line with our rapid growth, we will expand shareholder return policies to foster joint growth with our investors while making company-wide efforts to enhance corporate value as we strive to become a global big pharma leader.”

data.title: Seoul Guarantee Insurance soars over 15% on first day Kospi debut
data.description: Seoul Guarantee Insurance Co., a leading guarantee institution in South Korea, saw its stock price surge over 15 percent on the first day of trading on the country’s main Kospi market on Friday. The company’s stock was trading at 30,150 won ($20.7) as of 10:34 a.m. on Friday, up 15.96 percent from its initial offering price (IPO) of 26,000 won. The stock opened 7.88 percent higher at 28,050 won. The stock’s strong performance is driven by its dividend appeal. The company has committed to a total shareholder return of approximately 200 billion won per year over the next three years. Seoul Guarantee Insurance had planned to go public earlier but it withdrew its listing in October 2023 due to unfavorable market conditions. In the latest attempt, the company lowered its IPO price range from 39,500 won?51,800 won to 26,000 won?31,800 won, with the final IPO price set at the lower end of its desired range. The public subscription saw a lackluster competition ratio of 8.59 to 1.

data.title: Former Socar CEO launches tender offer for 170,000 Socar shares
data.description: Former Socar Inc. Chief Executive Officer Lee Jae-woong, the company’s largest shareholder, has launched a tender offer for approximately 170,000 shares. Industry sources said on Friday that Lee’s venture capital firm SOQRI announced plans to acquire 171,429 common shares (0.52 percent stake) of Socar at 17,500 won per share. The offer runs from Friday until April 2nd, 2025. SOQRI stated that the move aims to raise its stake to 19.72 percent, reinforcing responsible management and enhancing Socar’s competitiveness and corporate value. The tender offer is viewed as Lee taking a strategic step amid a potential management dispute with Socar’s second-largest shareholder, Lotte Rental Co., which holds a 25.7 percent stake. SOQRI currently owns 19.20 percent of Socar, and when this is combined with Lee’s personal 9.99 percent stake, CEO Park Jae-uk’s 2.98 percent, and shares held by related parties, Lee’s total influence is estimated at around 45 percent. Lotte Rental initially acquired an 11.81 percent stake in Socar in March 2022 before its Kospi listing and has since increased its holdings. Industry speculation suggests that Lotte Rental, which changed ownership this week from Lotte Group to private equity firm Affinity Equity Partners, could seek control of Socar and attempt to merge it with its own car-sharing service, G Car. Lee, well-known as one of South Korea’s first-generation platform entrepreneurs, founded the portal Daum in 1995. He became Socar’s CEO in 2018 but resigned in 2020 after the failed legalization of ride-hailing service Tada. Socar, Korea’s largest car-sharing company, recorded 2024 revenue of 431.8 billion won ($296.6 million) and an operating loss of 9.8 billion won. Market expectations for improved performance are rising, driven by the resumption of its used car sales business in the fourth quarter of 2024 and stronger profitability in its core operations. Socar’s stock closed at 14,210 won on Thursday.

data.title: Sagicor Financial Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Results and Announces 12.5% Dividend Increase
data.description:
(01:47 GMT) *Sagicor Financial Reports Fourth Quarter And Full Year 2024 Results And Announces 12.5% Dividend Increase >SFC.T
(01:47 GMT) Press Release: Sagicor Financial Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Results and Announces 12.5% Dividend Increase

Sagicor Financial Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Results and Announces 12.5% Dividend Increase

Canada NewsWire

TORONTO and BARBADOS, March 13, 2025

This news release for Sagicor Financial Company Ltd.
("Sagicor Financial", "Sagicor" or the "Company")
should be read in conjunction with the Company's Management's
Discussion & Analysis ("MD&A") and the Consolidated
Financial Statements for the period ended December
31, 2024. These documents are available on Sagicor's
website, at www.sagicor.com, under the heading "Financials
and Filings" and under Sagicor's profile at www.sedarplus.ca.
This news release presents non-IFRS measures used
by Sagicor in evaluating its results and measuring
its performance. These non-IFRS measures are not standardized
financial measures, are not included in the Consolidated
Financial Statements, and may not be comparable to
similar financial measures used by other companies.
They include annualised core return on shareholders'
equity, book value per share, core basic earnings
per share (core EPS), core dividend payout ratio,
core earnings, financial leverage ratio, Minimum Continuing
Capital and Surplus Requirement ("MCCSR") ratio, Group
Life Insurance Capital Adequacy Test ("Group-LICAT"),
net contractual service margin ("CSM"), net CSM to
shareholders, new business CSM, new business production,
net premium, return on shareholders' equity ("ROE"),
revenues, shareholders' equity plus net CSM to shareholders,
and total capital. See the "Non-IFRS and Other Financial
Measures" section in this document for relevant information
about such measures.

TORONTO and BARBADOS, March 13, 2025 /CNW/ - Sagicor Financial Company Ltd. , a leading financial services provider in Canada, the United States and the Caribbean, today announced its results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2024. All figures are in US$ unless otherwise stated.

Highlights

  • Core earnings(1) to shareholders of $28.0 million for Q4 increased 28% Y/Y, $90.9 million for 2024 increased 84% Y/Y
  • Core basic earnings per share(1) (EPS) of 20.4 for Q4 increased 33% Y/Y, 64.9 for 2024 increased 87% Y/Y
  • Core return on shareholders' equity(1) of 11.7% for Q4, 9.6% for 2024
  • Net income to shareholders of $52.4 million for Q4, $97.5 million for 2024
  • New business CSM(1) of $39.3 million for Q4, $166.3 million for 2024
  • Shareholders' equity of $959.7 million, with book value per share of US$7.08 or C$10.19
  • Shareholders' equity plus net CSM to shareholders(1) of $2.0 billion, or US$15.02 or C$21.61 per share
  • Financial leverage ratio(1) of 27.3%
  • Group-LICAT(1) ratio of 139%
  • Increase of 12.5% in quarterly dividend to US$0.0675 per common share to be paid during the second quarter of 2025 (US$0.27 annualised dividend)

Andre Mousseau, President and Chief Executive Officer, said:

"We are pleased to announce another solid quarter to end 2024. In our first full year of earnings including our Canadian segment, we recorded core earnings(1) to shareholders consistent with our guidance and reported net income to shareholders in excess of core earnings(1) to shareholders. Our annualized core ROE(1) in Q4 shows our potential for further earnings growth both in 2025 and in the medium term beyond. We continue to make meaningful progress on our strategic initiatives, including collaboration between our operating segments, refreshing our technology, and improving our access to and cost of capital, all with the aim to reduce costs, drive growth, and ultimately expand our return on shareholders' equity(1) ."

"We are also pleased to continue to expand our return of capital to shareholders. We repurchased 3.0 million shares in 2024 at a significant discount to book value, contributing to a 4% net reduction in share count for the year. This helped drive our book value per share higher than the rate of our retained earnings. Our increased projected core earnings,(1) robust capitalization and liquidity, and reduced share count are all enabling us to provide our shareholders with a 12.5% increase in our quarterly dividend. At this payment level we anticipate we will be at approximately the midpoint of our 30% to 40% target core dividend payout ratio(1) range for 2025. We are also issuing new guidance for 2025. While economic uncertainty may cloud certain macroeconomic variables, we believe our core initiatives will enable us to continue to grow our return on shareholders' equity(1) in 2025 and beyond."

Overall Sagicor Group — Financial Highlights

Sagicor's core results for 2024 improved significantly over 2023, with 2024 being the first full calendar year consolidating ivari as our Sagicor Canada segment. Core earnings(1) to shareholders of $90.9 million exceeded management's revised guidance from Q2 2024 of $80 million to $90 million, and new business CSM(1) of $166.3 million was within the provided range of $160 million to $180 million. Core basic earnings per share(1) grew by 87% Y/Y due to strong contributions from our Sagicor Canada segment.

Sagicor remains well capitalized with a Group-LICAT(1) ratio of 139% and a financial leverage ratio(1) of 27.3%.

Outlook and Medium-Term Targets

Sagicor is updating its guidance on key measures:*

  • Core basic EPS(1) for 2025 are expected to be approximately 74.0 to 80.0 per share;
  • New business CSM(1) for 2025 is targeted at $180 million to $200 million;
  • 2026 target for core earnings(1) to shareholders growth of over 10% above 2025;
  • Targeted core return on shareholders' equity (ROE)(1) over the medium-term of 13%+; and,
  • Targeted core dividend payout ratio(1) of 30% to 40%.
  • *Outlook and financial guidance is based on certain assumptions, including business, economic, and market conditions, as of the date hereof. Please refer to Sagicor's 2024 Annual MD&A for a discussion of material risks and assumptions.

Dividend Increase

On March 13, 2025, the Board of Directors of Sagicor Financial Company Ltd. approved and declared a quarterly dividend of US$0.0675 per common share, an increase of 12.5%. This quarterly dividend will be paid on April 17, 2025, to shareholders of record at the close of business on March 27, 2025.

Business Segment - Financial Highlights

Sagicor Canada

  • Sagicor Canada's sales production of $18 million for the quarter and $70 million for the year was consistent with management expectations, resulting in new business CSM of $11.7 million for the quarter and $45.9 million for the year.
  • Core earnings(1) to shareholders of $24.5 million for the quarter increased $3.4 million, or 16%, from the same quarter in the prior year, reflecting an increase in expected investment earnings that was partially offset by unfavorable mortality experience.
  • Core earnings(1) to shareholders was $86.9 million for the year.
  • Net income to shareholders of $7.8 million for the quarter was lower than core earnings(1) to shareholders due to unfavorable market-related impacts primarily from higher risk-free rates on surplus assets.
  • Net income to shareholders was $96.2 million for the year.
  • Net CSM(1) ended the year at $535.3 million, a decrease from the end of September 2024 due to unfavourable currency impact and changes in assumptions that offset organic CSM growth.

Sagicor Life USA

  • Sagicor Life USA's new business production(1) of $152 million for the quarter and $894 million for the year was lower than targeted production due to lower rates than competitors during a period of interest rate volatility. Sagicor believes that production will continue to vary quarter to quarter because of the dynamic nature of the market. For Q1 2025, Sagicor Life USA projects over $300 million of new business production.
  • Core earnings(1) to shareholders for the quarter of $10.6 million increased $1.1 million, or 12%, from the same quarter in the prior year, driven by higher net investment income on the growing investment portfolio.
  • Core earnings(1) to shareholders was $40.2 million for the year.
  • Net income to shareholders of $41.9 million for the quarter was higher than core earnings(1) to shareholders due to favorable market-related impacts and favorable tax recoveries.
  • Net income to shareholders was $51.6 million for the year.
  • Net CSM(1) decreased by $11.0 million to $154.5 million Q/Q due to basis changes from the previous quarter and the impact of the introduction of new reinsurance agreements.

Sagicor Jamaica

  • Sagicor Jamaica had strong net premium(1) growth across all business lines and improved margins from repricing of Group Health products. The commercial banking division continued its Y/Y growth trend in profit as a result of higher net investment income and fees, and the investment banking division reversed the prior year's unrealized capital losses leading to a meaningful improvement in profit.
  • Sagicor's share of Sagicor Jamaica's core earnings(1) to shareholders of $7.5 million for the quarter declined from $13.4 million for the same period in the prior year due to unfavorable experience and the impact of rising interest rates, offset by improved margins on core non-insurance activities and lower financing costs.
  • Sagicor's share of Sagicor Jamaica's core earnings(1) to shareholders was $31.1 million for the year.
  • Sagicor's share of Sagicor Jamaica's net income to shareholders of $10.4 million for the quarter declined from $17.3 million for the same period in the prior year due to marginally lower results from the long-term insurance and commercial banking divisions.
  • Sagicor's share of Sagicor Jamaica's net income to shareholders was $31.3 million for the year.
  • Net CSM(1) was $282.2 million, a slight increase Q/Q as strong new business CSM of $14.8 million was offset by changes in assumptions.

Sagicor Life

  • Sagicor Life's core earnings(1) to shareholders of $5.8 million for the quarter increased 21% over Q4 2023 reflecting improved profitability from short-term and long-term businesses from repricing and product-offering adjustments, and improved insurance experience and lower incidence of onerous contracts in the long-term business.
  • Core earnings(1) to shareholders was $26.3 million for the year.
  • Net income to shareholders of $12.4 million for the quarter was higher than core earnings(1) to shareholders primarily due to positive market experience.
  • Net income to shareholders was $38.5 million for the year.
  • Net CSM(1) was $247.7 million, increasing from $244.4 million at the end of September 2024 due to growth in organic CSM of $7.3 million driven by strong new business sales, partially offset by changes in actuarial assumptions of $3.8 million.

Head Office, Other and Adjustments

  • Core loss(1) to shareholders for Head Office, Other and Adjustments was $20.4 million for the quarter and $93.6 million for the year. The fourth quarter result benefitted from lower other interest and financing costs related to the refinancing of the term loan used to acquire ivari.
  • Net loss to shareholders was $20.1 million for the quarter and $120.1 million for the year.

Normal Course Issuer Bid

Sagicor repurchased a total of 118,800 shares in the fourth quarter. Of these, 112,200 shares were purchased and cancelled in Q4 2024 for a total cost of approximately US$0.5 million. The remaining 6,600 shares repurchased for a total cost of approximately US$0.03 million were not cancelled at the period end date. The number of issued and outstanding common shares as at December 31, 2024 was 135,541,348, net of treasury shares.

Annual Meeting of Shareholders

The annual meeting of shareholders will be held at 4:00 p.m. Eastern Daylight Time in Toronto (4:00 p.m. Atlantic Standard Time in Barbados and Trinidad and Tobago, 3:00 p.m. Eastern Standard Time in Jamaica) on Tuesday, May 13, 2025. The Company is holding the meeting as a hybrid meeting, which will be conducted in person and via live webcast.

Sagicor is using "notice-and-access" to deliver its meeting materials and, as a result, the notice of meeting and accompanying management information circular will be accessible on Sagicor's website at https://www.sagicor.com/en/investor-relations/annual-general-meeting, under Sagicor's profile on SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.ca, and at https://docs.tsxtrust.com/2173 from April 11, 2025. In advance of the meeting, shareholders are encouraged to review the information on the website including the proxy voting procedures.

The in-person meeting will take place at the Hilton Barbados Resort, Needhams Point, St. Michael, Barbados. Registered shareholders and duly appointed proxyholders wishing to attend the Meeting in person are encouraged to pre-register by sending an email to agm2025@sagicor.com and they will receive a response confirming their registration. Shareholders and duly appointed proxyholders who are unable to attend the Meeting in person may still attend virtually.

Registered shareholders and duly appointed proxyholders will be able to virtually attend, participate and vote at the meeting online at: https://web.lumiconnect.com/217655442 (password: sagicor2025 (all lower case, no spaces))

Non-IFRS and Other Financial Measures

The Company reports certain non-IFRS measures and insurance industry metrics that are used to evaluate its performance. As non-IFRS measures generally do not have a standardized meaning, they may not be comparable to similar measures presented by other companies. Securities regulators require such measures to be clearly defined and reconciled with their most comparable IFRS measures. These measures are provided as additional information to complement IFRS measures by providing further understanding of the results of the operations of the Company from management's perspective. Accordingly, these measures should not be considered in isolation, nor as a substitute for analysis of the Company's financial information reported under IFRS. Non-IFRS measures used to analyze the performance of the Company's businesses are set out below. Please see the discussion below for an explanation or a reconciliation of certain non-IFRS measures.

Minimum Continuing Capital and Surplus Requirements ("MCCSR"): Sagicor voluntarily adopted the MCCSR standard as its risk-based assessment measure to provide a consolidated view of capital adequacy. The MCCSR was a standard used by OSFI from 1992 until 2018, when it was superseded by LICAT. When it was in place, OSFI established a supervisory minimum Total Ratio of 150% under MCCSR. Canadian practices for calculation of the MCCSR evolved and changed from inception through its replacement. Sagicor has made certain interpretations in our calculation of the MCCSR, in consultation with our appointed actuary, which we believe appropriately reflect the risk-based assessment of our capital position, including accounting for CSM in MCCSR.

Group Life Insurance Capital Adequacy Test ("Group-LICAT"): The Group's goal is to maintain adequate levels, at sufficient margins above minimum regulatory capital requirements, to maintain consumer confidence as well as credit ratings with external rating agencies. Management engages the Board with regards to actions necessary to maintain appropriate capital levels. Sagicor has voluntarily elected to follow OSFI's LICAT Guideline, specifically the latest amendment which became effective January 1, 2025.

Return on Shareholders' Equity ("ROE"): IFRS does not prescribe the calculation of return on shareholders' equity and therefore a comparable measure under IFRS is not available. To determine this measure, reported net income/(loss) attributable to shareholders is divided by the average of common shareholders' equity at the start and end of the quarter, and annually, as the average of the quarterly average common shareholders' equity for the year. The quarterly return on shareholders' equity is annualised. This measure provides an indication of overall profitability of the company.

Annualised core return on shareholders' equity: This measures profitability using core earnings available to common shareholders as a percentage of the capital deployed to earn the core earnings. The Company calculates core ROE using average common shareholders' equity quarterly, as the average of common shareholders' equity at the start and end of the quarter, and annually, as the average of the quarterly average common shareholders' equity for the year. The quarterly Core return on shareholders' equity is annualised.

Book value per share: To determine the book value per share, shareholders' equity is divided by the number of shares outstanding at the period end, net of any treasury shares.

Revenues: Revenues is the sum of three IFRS measures: insurance revenue, net investment income, and fees and other income.

Financial leverage ratio: The financial leverage ratio is the ratio of notes and loans payable (refer to note 17 of the 2024 consolidated financial statements) to total capital, where total capital is defined as the sum of notes and loans payable, net contractual service margin and total equity. This ratio measures the proportion of debt the Company uses to finance its operations as compared with its capital.

Total capital: This measure provides an indicator for evaluating the Company's performance. Total capital ($3.5billion as at Q4 2024) is the sum of total shareholders' equity ($960 million), notes and loans payable ($954 million), non-controlling interests ($363 million) and net CSM ($1.2 billion). This measure is the sum of several IFRS measures.

New business CSM: This measure is the amount of the contractual service margin added from contracts initially recognized in the period, net of reinsurance.

New business production: This measure is equal to the amount of annuities and life insurance new business paid premium.

Net CSM: This measure is the balance of the direct contractual service margin net of reinsurance contractual service margin.

Net CSM to shareholders: This measure is the amount of the net CSM attributable to shareholders.

Net premium: The sum of premiums written by an insurance company, less premiums ceded to reinsurance companies, plus any reinsurance assumed in the reporting period, excluding segregated fund premium.

Organic CSM: It is the sum of the following components:

  • Impact of new insurance business ("impact of new business" or "new business CSM") is the impact from insurance contracts from insurance contracts initially recognized in the period. It includes the impacts related to policy cancellations and acquisition expenses, and it excludes the impacts of unusual new reinsurance contracts on in-force business which are considered management actions.
  • Expected movement related to finance income or expenses ("interest accretion") includes interest accreted on the CSM during the period and the expected change on VFA contracts if returns are as expected.
  • CSM recognized for service provided ("CSM amortization") is the portion of the CSM that is recognized in net income for service provided in the period; and
  • Insurance experience gains (losses) and other is primarily the change from experience variances that relate to future periods. This includes persistency experience and changes in future period cash flows caused by other current period experience (e.g., policyholder behavior that differs from expectations).

Shareholders' equity plus net CSM to shareholders: This measure is the sum of common shareholders' equity and Net CSM to shareholders. It is an important measure for monitoring growth and measuring insurance businesses' value.

Core basic earnings per share (Core Basic EPS): represents core earnings attributable to shareholders divided by the weighted average number of common shares outstanding. This is a measure to evaluate the Company's capacity to generate sustainable earnings.

Core dividend payout ratio: This is the ratio of dividends paid per share to core basic earnings per share.

Core earnings: Core earnings is intended to remove from reported earnings or loss the impacts of the following items that create volatility in Sagicor's results under IFRS, or that are considered to be not representative of Sagicor's business operating performance and long-term earnings potential including among others unexpected market-related impacts, changes in assumptions, management actions, certain acquisition or disposition related amounts and others such as one-time costs, amortization of intangibles, and tax effects of the aforementioned items. Other includes non-controlling interests on all the aforementioned items. Each of these items is classified as a supplementary financial measure and has no directly comparable IFRS financial measure disclosed in Sagicor's financial statements to which the measure relates, nor are reconciliations available. Pacific Current Extends AU$300 Million Share Buy-Back Deadline
data.description:
Pacific Current Group has extended the deadline for its off-market equal access share buy-back offer to March 21, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

data.title: Chartist Talks: These two stocks that can brighten your portfolio according to SBI Securities' Sudeep Shah
data.description:
Sudeep Shah of SBI Securities bullish on Kotak Mahindra Bank and Chennai Petroleum Corporation, which can add beautiful colours to the portfolio.

According to him, Kotak Mahindra Bank has been displaying strong relative outperformance against frontline indices over the past few trading sessions. Notably, the stock’s ratio chart against Nifty has given a decisive consolidation breakout, highlighting its growing relative strength, he said.

On Chennai Petroleum Corporation, he believes the stock has formed a Three Outside Up candlestick pattern on a daily scale, which is a strong reversal sign. Most noteworthy, the reversal is confirmed by robust volume, he said.

On the global front, considering the chart structure, the Deputy Vice President and Head of Technical and Derivative Research at SBI Securities believes the US indices (Dow Jones, S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite) are likely to extend their southward journey over the next few trading sessions.

Are the technical indicators suggesting a further fall in the Nifty FMCG index toward 45,000 before it starts an upward move?

From the recent low of 50,199 level, the Nifty FMCG index has witnessed a sharp pullback rally of 4.43 percent in just five trading sessions. However, since the last four trading sessions, the index has been oscillating in a narrow range of 1,143 points. On a weekly scale, it has formed a small body candle with long upper shadow. This show index has taken a breather after a pullback rally.

Most noteworthy, during the recent pullback, the index has failed to cross its 20-day EMA level. Further, the daily RSI (Relative Strength Index) also failed to sustain above 40 mark, which is a bearish sign as per RSI range shift theory.

Going ahead, if the index slips below the zone of 51,500-51,400, then the index may resume its southward journey. In that case, the zone of 51,000-50,900 will act as the next crucial support for the index. On the upside, if the index sustains above the 20-day EMA zone of 52,400-52,500 level, then we may witness an extension of pullback rally. In that case, the index is likely to test the level of 53,400, followed by 54,000 levels in the short term.

Do you have strong conviction that the Nifty 50 will decisively breakdown the 100-week EMA in the coming weeks?

Just as Holi brings a vibrant mix of colours, the market too has painted a dynamic picture over the past couple of trading sessions. In line with our expectations, the benchmark index staged a spirited pullback rally of over 700 points in just five trading sessions. However, on Monday, it hit a resistance zone at 22,670-22,700, where the confluence of the 20-day EMA and 38.2% Fibonacci retracement level of its prior downward journey formed a barrier—much like a playful splash of colours meeting a firm wall.

Since then, the index has been moving within a narrow 362-point range, reflecting a phase of consolidation rather than a breakout burst. Similar to how Holi celebrants pause before the next round of festivities, the market seems to be catching its breath. Technically, the index is likely to consolidate in the 22,700-22,200 zone in the near term. The daily RSI has been oscillating in the 42-39 range for the last six sessions, and the trend strength indicator, ADX (Average Directional Index) has been in a falling mode for seven sessions, signaling a lack of strong directional momentum.

We believe the ongoing pullback rally might still have a few more vibrant colours to splash on the market canvas. Talking about crucial levels, the index is likely to face an immediate hurdle at 22,670-22,700, as this zone marks a crucial resistance with the 20-day EMA and the 38.2% Fibonacci retracement level of its prior downward journey (23,807-21,965) is placed in that region. However, if the index manages to surpass this resistance, it could extend the upmove toward 23,000-23,300 levels in the coming sessions. On the downside, the 22,300-22,200 zone is likely to provide a cushion in case of any immediate decline.

As traders, much like Holi celebrants waiting for the perfect moment to strike, patience will be key. A clear breakout will set the stage for the next big move. Let's embrace this colourful pause and prepare for the next vibrant market move!

Do you foresee a new leg of upward movement in Solar Industries and further correction in Indus Towers?

The stock of Solar Industries has given a downward sloping trendline breakout on a daily scale. This breakout is confirmed by robust volume. Currently, the stock is trading above its short and long-term moving averages. The daily RSI is in bullish territory, and it is in rising mode. Hence, we believe the stock is likely to continue its northward journey.

The stock of Indus Towers has been consolidating within a narrow range of Rs 312-345 for the past 17 trading sessions. As a result, moving averages have started flattening, losing their curvy nature, indicating a lack of strong directional momentum. Additionally, the daily RSI remains in a sideways zone as per the RSI range shift rules, further confirming the ongoing consolidation. Given this chart structure, the stock is likely to continue trading within this range. A decisive breakout on either side will be key in determining the next trending move.

Which two stocks would add beautiful colors to the portfolio?

Kotak Mahindra Bank

The stock has been displaying strong relative outperformance against frontline indices over the past few trading sessions. Notably, the stock’s ratio chart against Nifty has given a decisive consolidation breakout, highlighting its growing relative strength. Recently, the stock broke out of a consolidation phase on the daily chart, accompanied by a sharp rise in volume, confirming the breakout’s credibility. Hence, we recommend accumulating the stock in the zone of Rs 1,990-1,970 level with a stop-loss of Rs 1,920. On the upside, it is likely to test the level of Rs 2,120 in the short term.

Chennai Petroleum Corporation

On a weekly scale, the stock has formed a Three Outside Up candlestick pattern on a daily scale, which is a strong reversal sign. Most noteworthy, the reversal is confirmed by robust volume. Further, the stock is surged above its 20 and 50-day EMA level. These averages are started edging higher, which is a bullish sign. The daily RSI is surged above 60 mark, and it is in rising mode. Hence, we recommend accumulating the stock in the zone of Rs 572-568 level with a stop=loss of Rs 545. On the upside, it is likely to test the level of Rs 620 in the short term.

Are the charts signaling more correction in the Dow Jones, S&P 500, and Nasdaq Composite?

Currently, all three major US indices are trading below their short and long-term moving averages. These averages are in falling mode, which is a bearish sign. Further, the daily RSI of all these indices is in bearish territory, reinforcing the prevailing weakness. Hence, considering this chart structure, we believe the indices are likely to extend their southward journey over the next few trading sessions.Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. data.title: Market Chatter: Doosan Enerbility Secures KRW2.2 Trillion Saudi Power Plant Deal
data.description:
South Korean heavy industrial company Doosan Enerbility secured a 2.2 trillion won engineering, procurement and construction (EPC) contract to build two 1,800-megawatt combined cycle power plants in Saudi Arabia, Yonhap News reported Thursday.

The Luma 1 and Nairiyah 1 projects are part of a broader contract won by a Korea Electric Power Corporation -led consortium, which includes Saudi Arabia's largest private independent power producer and the Saudi Electricity Company.

The deal was awarded via an international bid by the Saudi Power Procurement Company, the report said.

Shares of Doosan Enerbility rose nearly 2% in recent trade on Friday while those of Korea Electric Power increased 1%.

data.title: Democrats Clear Way for GOP Bill, Ending Threat of Shutdown — WSJ
data.description:
By Siobhan Hughes and Lindsay Wise

WASHINGTON — The leader of Senate Democrats moved to take the threat of a government shutdown off the table, following a grueling intraparty fight in which lawmakers struggled with how best to resist President Trump's fast-paced efforts to slim down federal agencies.

Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer (D., N.Y.) said he would vote to advance a Republican measure, set for Friday afternoon, to fund the government through the rest of the year. Earlier, in a closed-door lunch, he also said that enough Democrats would join him to help the Republicans clear the chamber's critical 60-vote hurdle, people familiar with the matter said.

"I will vote to keep the government open and not shut it down," Schumer said on the Senate floor, characterizing Democrats' alternatives as a Hobson's choice with no good option. He said the GOP proposal was bad, but the prospect of a shutdown was worse, giving Trump the "keys to the city, the state, and the country."

Democrats had struggled for days on how to approach the GOP bill, which provides the administration more flexibility to downsize and dismantle federal agencies, which Democrats adamantly oppose and Republicans have largely embraced. The funding measure had provided Democrats with a rare piece of leverage in the GOP-controlled Congress, and House Democrats and activists have been urging senators to use it to fight back.

"People are wrestling with, what is the least worst outcome?" said Sen. Martin Heinrich (D., N.M.), who said he would vote against the GOP bill. "I mean, I've made my own decision. I think when you confront a bully, you have to confront a bully."

But Democratic senators also worried that a shutdown, rather than forcing Republicans to the table, would simply play into Trump's hands, potentially giving him enhanced power to shutter more parts of the federal government for good, with no obvious way out.

In announcing his planned vote, Schumer said Trump was a nihilist who has "taken a blowtorch to our country" and warned that the president could decide "to cherry pick which parts of the government to reopen in a protracted shutdown."

A day earlier, Schumer had warned that there weren't, at that time, enough Democratic votes to advance the measure. He announced his stance after another long meeting of Senate Democrats.

Absent action from Congress, the government shutdown would start at 12:01 a.m. Saturday.

Republicans said Democrats needed to accept their situation and get on board with the bill, or risk what some GOP lawmakers dubbed a "Schumer shutdown." A shutdown would furlough hundreds of thousands of federal workers and restrict a range of government operations, although critical services would continue.

"If there's a shutdown, even the Democrats admit it will be their fault, " Trump said.

With Sen. Rand Paul (R., Ky.) already a "no," eight Democrats will be needed to vote "yes" to reach 60 votes. The House already passed the measure this week, but then left town, leaving no time for the Senate to revise it.

Democrats had pushed for an alternative plan to extend government funding for 30 days, to buy time to hammer out a routine omnibus spending bill that would clearly spell out that Congress has the constitutional power of the purse.

But the 30-day bill had no chance of passing the GOP-controlled Senate, leaving Democrats with the best-case scenario of an amendment vote that provides political cover but little else. Other Democrats called the proposal a sham.

"Total theater is neither honest with constituents nor a winning approach," fumed Sen. John Fetterman (D., Pa.), the only Senate Democrat to publicly say that he would support the GOP plan, in a post on X.

"Those games won't fool anyone. It won't trick voters, it won't trick House members. People will not forget it," said Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D., N.Y.), who urged Democratic senators to stand strong against the GOP plan, on X.

House Democrats stayed largely united in voting against the bill earlier this week. But unlike their Senate counterparts, they couldn't have blocked it.

Many Senate Democrats unleashed a wave of videos and statements detailing their opposition to the GOP resolution. Those opposed to both the measure and the critical procedural vote included Sens. Mark Warner (D., Va.), Adam Schiff (D., Calif.), Richard Blumenthal (D., Conn.), Ruben Gallego (D., Ariz.), and Mark Kelly (D., Ariz.).

But others, such as Sen. Jon Ossoff (D., Ga.), the most vulnerable Senate Democrat in the 2026 election, and Sen. Gary Peters (D., Mich.), who is retiring after his term ends in early 2027, kept their cards close to their vest.

For Democrats, a key issue is that the GOP's full-year continuing resolution omits the report that details where the executive branch should spend money, program by program. Such a report would give Democrats their best chance of defending the existence of shuttered operations in the courts.

This week alone, the Education Department announced that it was cutting about half of its staff and the Veterans Affairs Department outlined plans to cut about 70,000 from its workforce. That is on top of plans by the Social Security Administration to economize, which Democrats worry will make it harder for people to obtain benefits.

On Thursday, a federal judge ordered several federal agencies to reinstate thousands of probationary employees who were fired last month.

The continuing resolution would largely extend funding for fiscal 2025 at the prior year's levels, but with some key exceptions, including cutting nondefense spending by $13 billion and increasing military spending by $6 billion.

It would further unravel former President Joe Biden's signature Inflation Reduction Act, taking another $20.2 billion away from the portion of Internal Revenue Service funds raised in that law to increase tax compliance. The repeat cut — which essentially doubles the cut contained in the fiscal 2024 spending law — would almost entirely reverse the enforcement boost that the IRS got in 2022.

The spending measure also forces Washington, D.C., to make more than $1 billion in cuts.

Write to Siobhan Hughes at Siobhan.hughes@wsj.com and Lindsay Wise at lindsay.wise@wsj.com

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

2151 ET - Hong Kong's benchmark Hang Seng Index rises 0.4% to 23548.43, led by gains in consumer and tech stocks. Markets remain firmly focused on trade tariff news, and sentiment towards risk assets are starting to weaken as U.S. tariffs start to take effect against China, Mexico and Canada, and other metal imports, the UOB Global Economics & Markets Research team writes in a note. Among the gainers, China Mengniu Dairy rises 4.7%, Baidu gains 2.8% and Alibaba Group is 1.8% higher. The decliners include CK Hutchison, which is down 5.9%, WH Group off 3.4% and AIA Group, which is 2.4% lower. The Hang Seng Tech Index is down 0.1% at 5741.10. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

2132 ET - DFI Retail Group looks positioned for earnings growth in 2025, UOB Kay Hian's Adrian Loh says in a research report. For 2025, the Asian retailer guided for 2% revenue growth and projected underlying profit of US$230 million-US$270 million, the analyst notes. The Singapore-listed company expects to generate this growth from its health & beauty and convenience segments and from optimizing its product mix to improve margins, the analyst says. Also, the company disclosed it'll seek to monetize data from its "yuu" platform and advertising opportunities within its supermarkets, among other initiatives. The brokerage raises the stock's target price to US$2.80 from US$2.57 and maintains a buy rating. Shares are unchanged at US$2.25. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2120 ET - Mr. D.I.Y. Group (M) could face a potential exclusion from Malaysia's benchmark Kuala Lumpur Composite Index in the semiannual review this June, Affin Hwang IB analyst Low Jing Yuan says in a note. The company's market capitalization ranks 34th after a recent selloff, falling outside the top-30 threshold for index inclusion, he says. Shares are 29% lower year-to-date. Its aggressive store expansion appears to have reached a saturation point, with diminishing revenue contribution per outlet and rising operating costs likely to pressure margins. The weak 3Q and 4Q results last year reflected these challenges, he says. Affin Hwang cuts the stock's target price to MYR1.10 from MYR1.25, on downside risks from slowing growth and potential index removal, and keeps a sell rating. Shares are 2.2% lower at MYR1.32. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

2112 ET - Singapore's FTSE Straits Times Index edges 0.2% lower to 3831.46, tracking Wall Street's losses overnight. The main theme has been risk-off, Commerzbank Research analysts say in a research report. There are worries over further escalation in the trade war, the analysts say, noting President Trump's threat to impose 200% tariff on U.S. imports of wine, champagne and other alcoholic beverages from the EU. Among worst performers on the benchmark index, Sembcorp Industries falls 2.6%, Mapletree Logistics Trust loses 0.8%, and OCBC is down 0.7%. Meanwhile, Wilmar International rises 0.6% and index heavyweight DBS adds 0.4%. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2107 ET - Malaysia's benchmark Kuala Lumpur Composite Index is 1.0% lower at 1495.59. With global markets unsettled ahead of the coming reciprocal tariffs, profit-taking in the local bourse may occur after Thursday's rebound, Apex Securities says in a note. Persistent foreign fund outflows also add to the volatility, the brokerage reckons. It maintains a defensive stance, advising a wait-and-see approach until trade policy clarity emerges post-April. Among the decliners, Sunway Bhd. is down 2.3% and Public Bank is 2.2% lower. Meanwhile, Maxis Bhd. adds 0.6% and YTL Power International is 0.3% higher. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

2016 ET - Japanese stocks are lower in early trade as concerns about U.S. tariffs weigh. Tech and chemical stocks are leading the declines. Recruit Holdings is down 4.4% and Shin-Etsu Chemical is 1.5% lower. USD/JPY is at 147.88, compared with 147.64 as of Thursday's Tokyo stock-market close. Investors are paying attention to developments related to U.S. tariffs and Japanese monetary policy. The Nikkei Stock Average is down 0.1% at 36742.68. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

2015 ET - Australian lithium miner Liontown Resources delivered an in-line result, with revenue and cash flow largely pre-reported, say Barrenjoey analysts. First-half underlying Ebitda of A$66.0 million was well above of Barrenjoey and consensus estimates "given LTR took the approach of capitalizing all costs over and above revenue to effectively create a nil gross margin," the analysts say. Commercial production at the Kathleen Valley processing plant was declared effective Jan. 1, and capitalization of costs will stop from that date, they say. Depreciation was higher-than-anticipated, but net debt was as expected, say the analysts. They continue to expect Liontown will meet 2H FY25 guidance. Liontown is up 0.4% at A$0.6175. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

1957 ET - Macquarie upgrades a string of Australian mining stocks, citing a more-positive view on gold, iron ore and lithium-rich spodumene prices. It also downgrades a few--all coal-mining stocks, with the thermal-coal outlook having especially soured, according to Macquarie analysts. In a note, the analysts say South32 and BHP are Macquarie's top picks among Australia's diversified miners, and are preferred over Fortescue and Rio Tinto. Newmont is its large-cap gold pick and IGO its top bet for lithium, while Mineral Resources "offers high reward for risk," they say. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

1948 ET - Japanese stocks may trade rangebound as uncertainty over U.S. tariffs continues. Nikkei futures are flat at 36560 on the SGX. USD/JPY is at 147.92, compared with 147.64 as of Thursday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are focusing on developments related to U.S. tariffs, as well as Japanese monetary policy ahead of the Bank of Japan's policy meeting next week. The Nikkei Stock Average fell 0.1% to 36790.03 on Thursday. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com)

1946 ET - Conditions look as good as they will get for Australian lenders mortgage insurer Helia, says Macquarie, downgrading its stock to underperform from neutral. While low claims are likely in the near term, Macquarie doesn't think the trend of negative claims is sustainable. Also, Macquarie cites risks around the renewal of a contract with Commonwealth Bank of Australia. It doesn't think investors are pricing in the potential of the contract being lost. "Trading at 1.6x price-to-net tangible assets, the valuation is expensive and does not reflect potential risks, in our view," Macquarie says. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1938 ET - The 20% drop in Lifestyle Communities' share price since Feb. 24 looks excessive to Citi, which upgrades the retirement village operator to buy, from neutral. Citi says it understands the uncertain sales and earnings outlook for FY 2026, but outlines reasons to be upbeat. "The steps taken by the company in February highlighted a firm commitment to balance sheet strength, which along with an improving environment (rate cuts), and a new CEO is a positive, in our view," Analyst Suraj Nebhani says. Citi also believes Lifestyle Communities remains a potential takeover target. The implied value of Lifestyle Communities' development business is between 3-8X FY 2024 EBIT, representing a big discount to peer Ingenia Communities, Citi says. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1937 ET - Citi turns bullish on property company Charter Hall, citing the potential for a recovery in its earnings as asset values stabilize, as well as a return of equity flows into Australia. "The stock trades at 18.5x FY 2026 earnings which is above average, but earnings are near trough as they include minimal transaction and performance fees," analyst Suraj Nebhani says. Also, multiples tend to expand in periods of growth in funds under management, signaling a positive outlook, Citi adds. It upgrades Charter Hall to buy, from neutral. It retains a A$18.50/share price target on Charter Hall, which is up 0.5% at A$16.13 in early trading today. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.description:
(Updates to add WiseTech's statement in the sixth and seventh paragraphs)

WiseTech Global appointed consultancy firm Sodali & Co to conduct an investor survey on its governance practices, following the resignation of its chairman and three independent directors, clearing the way for founder Richard White's return as executive chairman, the Australian Financial Review reported Thursday.

White's comeback follows controversy over his personal life, including allegations of inappropriate conduct linked to an attempt to bankrupt a former lover.

A board review into White's behavior sparked disputes over its disclosure, prompting last month's director exit.

Over the past week, Sodali has conducted confidential interviews with major shareholders, with results expected to guide the board's decisions, per the report.

WiseTech currently breaches Australian Securities Exchange (ASX) rules requiring audit committees to have at least three non-executive directors but plans to address this within four weeks of releasing its February results.

In an emailed statement to MT Newswires, a WiseTech spokesperson confirmed the shareholder engagement process to get their views on the relevant skillset for the future board composition.

The company's lead independent director met lawyers at Seyfarth Shaw regarding the board review, the spokesperson added.

data.title: DATA BREACH ALERT: Edelson Lechtzin LLP Is Investigating Claims On Behalf Of Imagine360, LLC Customers Whose Data May Have Been Compromised
data.description:
NEWTOWN, Pa., March 13, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Edelson Lechtzin LLP, a national class action law firm, is investigating claims regarding data privacy violations at Imagine360, LLC (“Imagine360”). Imagine360 learned of suspicious activity on its network on or about March 10, 2024. To join this case, go HERE.

About Imagine360, LLC

Imagine360 is a healthcare solutions provider that partners with self-funded employers to reduce costs and enhance the member experience. Their comprehensive services include expert plan management, dedicated member support, and cost-saving strategies.

What happened?

On or around March 10, 2024, Imagine360 detected suspicious activity involving employee email accounts. As a result, they initiated an investigation, which determined that an unauthorized third party around the same date may have accessed certain files containing sensitive personal information. These files potentially included personal information.

What type of information was stolen?

The personal information in the compromised files may have included:

  • Names
  • Social Security Numbers
  • Driver’s License Numbers or State Issued Identification Numbers
  • Financial Information (Financial Account Numbers, Credit/Debit Card Number)
  • Medical Information
  • Taxpayer ID Number
  • Identity Protection Personal ID Number

How can I protect my personal data?

If you receive a data breach notification concerning Imagine360, you must guard against possible misuse of your personal information, including identity theft and fraud, by regularly reviewing your account statements and monitoring your credit reports for suspicious or unauthorized activity.

Edelson Lechtzin LLP is investigating a class action lawsuit to seek legal remedies for individuals whose sensitive personal data may have been compromised by the Imagine360 data breach.

data.title: SF REIT Swings to Loss in 2024
